entry.title: Structured Goal Plan with Goals under Category and Metrics.
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have a requirement to structure the Goal Plan in Successfactors in the below format:

  1. 1st Level is to have the Category (Customer,Stakeholder)
  2. 2nd Level is to have the Metrics which is dynamic based on the functions and can change for different employees.
  3. Add the Goal so that it shows under the specific metric.

The Goal Alignment feature also doesn't help in achieving this requirement.

Can you please confirm if this is achievable in Successfactors as a structure in the Goal form or Goal Plan.

Also, can we have a concept of a parent goal and a sub-goal under that.

entry.content:
But will it run Crysis?

 

 

 

 

Nice Job lol

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for May - ABAP Cloud
entry.content:
Escape done thanks to #ABAPcloud !!!

I really enjoyed this May Challenge, thanks @sheenamk for all your effort to motivate the community to learn with fun activities.

entry.title: Passed C_SIGBT_2409 – Seeking Advice on Next SAP Certification
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m happy to share that I’ve passed the SAP Certified Associate - Business Transformation Consultant (C_SIGBT_2409) exam.

For my preparation, I relied on a combination of trusted resources:

  • Official SAP Materials – including SAP Learning Hub, SAP Signavio documentation, and relevant OpenSAP courses, which provided a strong foundation in process intelligence, modeling, and integration topics

  • P2Pcerts – offered well-structured practice tests and scenario-based questions that helped assess and reinforce my readiness

  • Community Resources – such as YouTube tutorials and SAP Community blogs, which gave practical insights and real-world examples

My focus areas included the SAP Signavio suite, business process governance, transformation strategies, and integration with SAP S/4HANA.

Now, I’m looking to continue building on this knowledge. Could you recommend a suitable next certification, ideally aligned with business transformation, process excellence, or SAP S/4HANA?

entry.title: Re: Recurrence process in Convergent Charging
entry.content:
Hello,

the activation (i.e. the computation of the recurring fee) is always done before the rating of the consumption item. 

Indeed, this is a mandatory step to be sure that the provider contract is up to date before the rating of the consumption item. A the reception of a rating request, SAP CC activates the provider contract until the consumption date which is provided in the rating request. 

It is not possible to bypass this process. 

Best regards,

Eric .F

entry.title: Re: Error "Duplicate column names in the result" with col...
entry.content:
I do not get this error when selecting two objects that reference two tables with the same column name.  So I am guessing that you are coding this SQL in a universe derived table.  Please correct me if I am wrong.

When coding a derived table, as you mentioned, you need to provide a unique alias, like TABLE_1.COL_NAME as COL_NAME1.  Help me to understand why this would not be sustainable.  If you are coding a derived table, and then testing the results, you will instantly know if you need to add qualifying column names.

I can see there being an issue if you code a SELECT * within a derived table but this is a bad practice for several reasons.  One is that if a new column is added to a table and it has a generic name, you could run into the issue you mention.  Second reason is that pulling in more columns than needed into a sub-query requires more temp database space when used within a report.

This really isn't an answer because I don't see it as a problem.  If you can provide more details/explanation, it might help me understand the issue.

entry.title: Re: Upgrade from BI 4.2 to BI 4.3 - Which Service Pack and patch should be used?
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm planning a move from BI4.2 to BI4.3 SP4 too.

I'm hesitating between FP10 and FP11.

Can you recommend which FP I should apply?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: MyInbox tasks count error
entry.content:
3613131 - dynamic tiles counters error in federated apps in sap fiori launchpad

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003613131

As explained there you need to go to the channel manager and save again the Runtime Destination for Dynamic Data.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Application Generator Available in Open Source
entry.content: 2021 ... that really has been a while now. 😅 Awesome to finally see this huge undertaking become fully OSS and see the pieces "fall in place" over the past years. Though I honestly believed it already was "fully" open source since the repo wasn't a "big bang" publish and available for quite some time now. Regardless, congratulations to each and everyone involved! 🎉🥳

entry.title: Beyond Accessibility Compliance – Toward Inclusion
entry.content:
A field of red and purple tulips, with a single red tulip with white trim prominently in focus

 

How SAP is Working to Make Business Software More Accessible and Inclusive

“Wouldn’t it be great if users could access software and its functionality as naturally and efficiently as a hummingbird gets nectar from a flower?”  We posed this question more than ten years ago in one of our most widely read articles on software accessibility at SAP. We continue to be motivated by the same belief: software should be intuitive and simple for everyone, including people with disabilities. And ideally, with even less effort than it takes a hummingbird to reach its nectar.

Why Accessibility Matters

In the digital world, accessibility matters because it helps ensure that people with disabilities can do the things many take for granted: participate in online learning, pursue employment and career opportunities, use online services, engage on social media, enjoy live and recorded events, and much more. Accessibility enables people with disabilities to live independently, stay connected, and fully participate in our increasingly digital world.

But accessibility isn’t just about convenience, features, or design – it’s a matter of human rights. The United Nations Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (CRPD) makes clear that people with disabilities have the same fundamental rights as everyone else, including the right to access information, technology, education, and public services. Accessibility is what helps those rights to be fully exercised in today’s technology-driven world. This principle is also reflected in many national laws and software procurement regulations around the world.

In the sections that follow, we’ll explore how accessibility fits into the global conversation around inclusion and human rights, and how it shapes the way we think, design, and build products at SAP. You’ll also find a section with links to our latest resources.

Who Accessibility Supports

Not everyone has experience with disability, whether personally or through a friend or family member. Still, data helps show the broad benefit of accessibility in people’s lives, even in ways we might take for granted or not expect. According to the World Health Organization (WHO), about 16% of the global population – roughly 1.3 billion people – live with a significant disability. That number is growing due to population growth and rising rates of non-communicable diseases. Beyond the 1.3 billion people, many more experience everyday challenges that affect how they interact with digital tools and platforms.

Vision Impairment. At least 2.2 billion people worldwide have some form of vision impairment, ranging from near and distance vision issues to significant vision loss, including blindness. Some are counted among persons with disabilities, while others are not. But all can benefit from accessible designs. Accessibility features like zoom and high-contrast settings can make everyday tasks easier for some. For others, screen readers and keyboard navigation are essential.

Hearing Loss. Globally, more than 1.5 million people experience some degree of hearing loss, a number expected to grow to 2.5 billion by 2050. As with vision, not all forms of hearing meet the legal definition of a disability. As such, some are counted as a person with a disability while others are not. Still, accessibility features designed to support people with hearing loss often improve the digital experience for everyone. Features like captions, mute controls, and vibration notifications are helpful in many everyday situations – from noisy environments to shared or quiet spaces where audio isn’t ideal. They also support people experiencing temporary hearing conditions, such as tinnitus. What began as assistive technology has become a standard part of digital design and one that benefits everyone.

Neurodiversity. A variety of studies estimate that 15-20% of the global population is neurodivergent. While neurodiversity isn’t a disability, some related conditions are recognized as disabilities under certain laws. As with vision and hearing differences, not everyone in this group is considered a person with a disability. Still, accessibility and personalization options, like theming options and text spacing, can help people focus and work in ways that are best for them. As with other accessibility features, they help improve digital experiences for everyone.

Accessibility helps not only the 1.3 billion people with disabilities; it helps all of us. Any of us can face a disability, whether from illness, injury, age, or daily situations. Accessibility features support everyone, no matter our abilities or needs.  At SAP, this broader view of accessibility shapes how we design, test, and build software every day.

20 Years of Accessibility at SAP

In 2024, SAP Accessibility marked 20 years! Over the decades, we’ve continually updated our internal guidance, development processes, and testing practices to reflect evolving technologies, device innovations, regulations, and user needs. Innovations in test automation and software engineering continue to play a key role in helping us improve the quality of our products.

As a global leader in business software, SAP supports customers worldwide in their buying decisions by providing Accessibility Conformance Reports on a product level. These reports are based on the Voluntary Product Accessibility Template (VPAT) developed by the Information Technology Industry Council (ITI), which provides a standardized format that helps customers compare accessibility information across products. Each report indicates the extent to which an SAP product supports WCAG at the requirement level. The Accessibility Conformance Reports are available in three formats:

  • WCAG 2.0 (U.S.)
  • WCAT 2.1/EN 201 549
  • WCAG 2.2

We focus not only on improving compliance but also on creating an efficient user experience for people with disabilities. That’s why accessibility is considered early in the design process, together with usability recommendations. This approach is supported by our Accessibility Design Tools Handbook and Inclusive Research Handbook, which are part of our software creation process and freely available for others to use. The SAP Design System is built on this guidance and services as the foundation for the next evolution of our software.

Accessibility is a journey – one that evolves with every new technology and innovation. Advances like AI continue to shape how we design and build, and we’re committed to making that progress include everyone.

Explore More from SAP Accessibility

To learn more about our efforts to build more intuitive and inclusive experiences, check out these resources.

Stay Connected:

  • SAP Accessibility Community

Resources & Blogs:

  • SAP Accessibility Design Tools Handbook
  • SAP Inclusive Research Handbook
  • Beyond Checklists: Growing Our Inclusive Experience Mindset

Compliance & Support:

  • SAP Software Accessibility (SAP Trust Center)
  • SAP Note 3260235 - SAP Specifics Regarding Accessibility Conformance (available to all SAP customers through your support portal)

entry.title: SAP Activate Exam SAP System Provisioning Order
entry.content:
Hello

In the SAP Activate Exam SAP 2403 comes out this question:

What is the right order when provisioning systems in Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

1.Set Up SAP Cloud Identity Services
2.Set Up SAP Cloud ALM
3.Set Up SAP Central Business Configuration (Evaluation Workspace)
4.Set Up the Starter System (Customizing Tenant and Development Tenant) in a 3-System Landscape or 
5. Set Up the Starter System in a 2-System Landscaps

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/507d742f60b1418190cbe3c60d577b4b.html?locale=en-US

Now, it looks to me that in differnt  places the order changes where Starter System (Ans 4) looks to be first as the correct answer in SAP Activate Exam.

Please some clarifications on this, sap activate exam as of today what is the right order?

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/implementing-sap-s-4-hana-cloud-public-edition/provisioning-systems-and-initial-setup

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/implementing-sap-s-4-hana-cloud-public-edition/exploring-system-landscapes

entry.title: Re: Destination Error - "Internal Server Error" - CAP Project HTML5-App-repository
entry.content: Hi @lukasmetzger, I have the same issue right now. When deployed, I have the error that my application is missing the xs-app.json and it seems this is because in the build process it is not copied. How did you do it? How did you change the build process so this issue is fixed? Many thanks!

entry.title: Question on applicability of Security Note 3561861
entry.content:
Should we still apply the correction instructions to mitigate the vulnerability according to SAP Note 3561861 in SAP Solution Manager?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Question on applicability of Security Note 3561861
entry.content: As a SolMan system also has a role like CRM system type, it is important to implement SAP Note #3561861 by applying the corrections for BBPCRM and WEBCUIF accordingly. This will ensure that the SolMan system functions effectively and aligns with CRM system requirements.

entry.title: Re: Text is not visible in lower level of level based hierarchy with compounded key
entry.content: Hi Shlomi, only hierarchy with directory needs own texts. The standard hierarchy uses the same texts as the view self. Means it must work. I usually use associations for texts, not directly joined into the view (development style and the texts work without an issue. I do not have any texts fields in the hierarchy self (only when working with hierarchy with directory). Try to post some screen shots of the views. Martin

entry.title: Applying Credit Memo for RMA to original Sales Order
entry.content: I am working on trying to revamp the RMA process for my current company.  After the return sales order and credit memo for RMA have been created, we need to know the process to relieve the revenue amount off of the original sales order.  We see revenue being reversed on the 6 series credit memo document, but it is not reducing the revenue from the original sales order that is referenced to the credit memo.  MR11 is being used to reduce cost of goods sold from the SO that has had the RMA, but we are unable to figure out how to systematically reduce the revenue.  Because SAP is seeing all revenue as billed, but COGs have been backed out due to MR11, SAP is systematically deferring revenue that it shouldn't.  Any help would be appreciated.

entry.title: Re: How to fix time out issues where tracking won't allow...
entry.content: The system has a 30min. hard coded timeout. The details of the KBA 2088893 can be reviewed. After the 30min. with no ping or no internet, if for some reason the user doesn't see the logout/timeout on screen, the browser logs should confirm it.

entry.title: Re: Error message displayed on return purchase order(Scop...
entry.content:
Hello, 

Please find below SAP Note, that intorudces steps to take care of when such error appears: 

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002982468

Please let me know if it helped! 

Regards, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Re: list of standard OCN which is supported by SAP SF.
entry.content: @itsme, there's no specific list of OCN providers. Providers must adhere to the guidelines outlined in the Implementing Open Content Network guide. Some providers can be found on the SAP Store, although the total number of providers isn't limited to those listed on the site.

entry.title: Re: DEACTIVATE SOCIETY PURCHASE ACCOUNT
entry.content:
Hello, 

Please find other Community Topics regarding Purchasing Account Management in SAP Public Cloud. They might intoruce you closer to the topic:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/how-to-activate-purchase-account-in-sap-cloud/qaq-p/12544617

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-members/fi-mm-purchase-account-management/ba-p/13549606

The configuration steps are desribed above, it will be possible to deactivate it in this configuration.

Hope it helps you!

Regards, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Re: Sap Public Cloud - auto population of the Confirmatio...
entry.content:
Hello, 

Please find below SAP Note about custom control key: 

https://me.sap.com/notes/3143065/E

Here you can find Note about control key in sheduling agreements:

https://me.sap.com/notes/2868471/E

Here you will find path to Master Data:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f86dc2eb1f8b48c880a7607213104b27/0b48f0f1a9694e15b04e66c365e5dcf8.html?locale=en-US&q=confirmation+control+key+

Here you will find information about extending it in CDS View:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/33192c1211354eed9e40fac66e55fcc1.html?locale=en-US&q=confirmation+control+key+

Hope you will find it helpful. Do not hesitate to ask further questions!

Regards, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Re: Supplier/vendor Evaluation Process in SAP S/4HANA with Questionnaire Guide
How to cancel material document created by batch program
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

On the production system, a material document is created by the Batch Program. The user wants to cancel this transaction, using MIGO Movement type 108 (created by 107), but he getting authorization issue. What is the procedure to cancel this transaction. Please advise.

 

Kashif

entry.title: Re: Supplier/vendor Evaluation Process in SAP S/4HANA with Questionnaire Guide
entry.content:
Hi @Marco_Rossi  ,

Thanks for your question!

Yes, in SAP it is possible to send a supplier evaluation questionnaire directly to the supplier using a link via email, especially if you're using SAP Ariba or SAP S/4HANA with Supplier Lifecycle Management (SLC). The supplier can open the link and complete the form themselves online.

Alternatively, in some setups, you can also send the form internally to an appraiser who completes the evaluation on behalf of the supplier. This depends on how your system is configured.

The email link method is the easiest and most efficient if you're using SAP Ariba or SLC.
Let me know what system you're using, and I can guide you further.

Regards,
Leelamohan

entry.title: Re: Queue Contents Editing for Failed Queues from EWM to ERP
entry.content:
Hello @shivaewm 

Thanks a lot for the detailed explanation. But we have a requirement to add additional field names in the QRFC FM structures

Eg:

Qrfc FM:BAPI_OUTB_DELIVERY_CONFIRM_DEC

Qrfc param name: ITEM_DATA 

Field name: SLOC, QTY, BATCH

Those fields are not shown in F4 List but we have a requirement to add those fields so that those fields should be editable  in Queues. Could you please let me know if this is possible in S/4 system and if then please could you provide the steps to do so.

Regards,

Veer

entry.title: Create custom tab and add fields in /SCWM/PACK transaction in S/4 EWM
entry.content:
Hello All,

We have a requirement to create custom tab in /SCWM/PACK transaction in EWM where we can send the update from EWM to S4 to update the same custom fields also how those fields could be stored in SAP Table.

Can anyone please share on steps on how to implement the same as i'm unable to find any blog in SAP.

 

Regards,

Veer

entry.title: Re: Custom Fields in BSEG not updating on FI document post
entry.content: It worked also for me! Really appreciated that you posted this. Thank you!

entry.title: RELEASE STRATEGY in PURCHASE REQUISITION AT header level not getting displayed
entry.content:
Hi Gurus, Release strategy tab is not showing after check in service PR though it is working for material PR.
It is working fine in QAS but facing issue only in DEV.I have checked the configuration part along with functional in both DEV and QAS
and everything seems correct .

HEADER WISE NO ERRORS


I have compared the code in both DEV and QAS for EXIT_SAPLEBND_001 . Both are same. But still release strategy tab is not coming
in DEV.
Recently we have got requirement to make SRVPOS field mandatory in service PR  for which I have implemented 4 methods

 

 


IF_EX_ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST~FIELDSELECTION_HEADER_REFKEYS
FIELDSELECTION_HEADER
FIELDSELECTION_ITEM_REFKEYS
FIELDSELECTION_ITEM

 


I was working on all these 4 methods where I was trying to make the SRVPOS field mandatory which was not successful.
However, the functional had made it mandatory from the configuration.
I have commented all the code in 4 methods.

Once the release strategy issue started, I have mapped the import and export
parameters expecting the release strategy would work. but it did not.
Now the functional has reversed the changes of making the SRVPOS field mandatory as not required . but the issue not fixed.
Both functional and as technical from my end everything seems fine but Release strategy tab is not popping at header in DEV though
it is working fine in QAS.
I was trying to debug the standard code whole day but I am unable to find some solution.
Please help me how to get the release strategy tab in DEV .

entry.title: Re: Intercompany project accruals
entry.content:
Hello @lauri90 

Thank you for sharing your question

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, intercompany accruals and deferrals for projects are not supported in supplying company codes. Revenue recognition occurs solely within the project-owning entity, which limits cross-company margin visibility.

Workarounds include manual adjustments during period-end close and using SAP Group Reporting or SAC for consolidated reporting.

Let me know if you need further support

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Creating Approval Process flow in SAP Service Cloud V2

entry.title: Re: Error in Customer Master Migration in Migrate your Data
entry.content:
Hi @danny_loo 

This error is explained in the following KBA, could you please have a look at that and test again by following the recommendation on the document?

KBA 2897577 - R1658 error during migration of Customer or Supplier

Best regards,
Irene

entry.title: Re: Middle Name in SAP Public cloud
entry.content:
Hi @vk36 

You are correct, Middle name is not there in Excel template and from S4 side there is no other way to do this bulk data posting.

Some of other channels like SOAP API have this field, while it is a different integration channel all together and I don't think customer would opt for this integration unless there is a external HR system involved. 

Best regards,
Irene

entry.title: Complying to EUDR using SAP Ariba Supplier Management and SAP Green Token
entry.content:
Authors: Allan Soo, Sabrina Haller, Gloria Figaroa and Nike Klaubert

Complying to European Union Deforestation Regulation using SAP Ariba Supplier Management and SAP Green Token

Introduction

From the 30th of December 2025, large and medium-sized EU importers will be impacted by the European Union Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) requiring them to conduct both due diligence and track and trace activities to make sure that the commodity (produced after the date that the regulation comes into force) does not originate from a plot of land that has been cleared for commercial purposes. Initially announced from 29th of June 2023, the phase-in period gives companies time to prepare for the regulation.

What commodities and products are impacted?

EUDR declares that both raw commodities and products containing these commodities are subject to compliance. The commodities include:

  • Cattle, meat livestock and leather
  • Cocoa beans, cocoa powder (no sugar/sweeteners) and foods containing cocoa
  • Coffee
  • Palm oil and some of its derived products
  • Rubber, including tyres, conveyor belts and cords
  • Soya beans
  • Wood and timber, including pallets, kitchenware, printed paper, furniture and seats.

Based on these items, it becomes difficult to determine whether the item requires EUDR due diligence assessments. Purchasing activities from foreign countries requires determining whether the impacted commodity contains a product that is restricted from imports into the EU. Further details on these commodities can be viewed in the EUDR document, Annex I.

Who should be concerned?

Sourcing and procurement departments in the EU will need to begin implementing processes that conduct thorough due diligence and reporting activities to ensure that imported items are not subject to deforestation for commercial purposes.

 A large company, according to the EU Accounting Directive (2013/34/EU), would exceed two of the following:

  • Balance sheet total of €20,000,000
  • Net turnover of €40,000,000
  • Average number of employees of 250 for the financial year

For medium sized undertakings, the entity must not exceed two of the following:

  • Balance sheet total of €20,000,000
  • Net turnover of €40,000,000
  • Average number of employees of 250 for the financial year

The commission, furthermore, defines small undertakings as:

  • Firms with fewer than 50 employees for the financial year
  • Annual turnover or balance sheet below €10 million

And micro undertakings as:

  • Firms with fewer than 10 employees
  • With an annual turnover below €2 million for the financial reporting year.

EUDR reporting periods begin with large and medium enterprises from the 30th of December 2025, with small and micro enterprises beginning their reporting periods from 30th of June 2026. Any new item containing an EUDR commodity that is being placed onto the EU market from these dates will be subject to the regulation. Entities are to conduct their EUDR due diligence checks once every year and store these records.

What about Omnibus Proposal proposed by the EU Commission?

The proposed Omnibus package will not amend current planned obligations to existing and future reporting entities. Leaving EUDR largely intact.

Penalties for noncompliance?

Up to 4% of the importer’s annual turnover and confiscation of commodities or products if it is found to originate from a plot of land cleared for commercial purposes. For wood products, the exporter must have legally obtained the product for export into the EU.

Procurement’s role in addressing EUDR

Situations that require procurement to have processes in place for complying to EUDR includes purchasing a new item from any (existing or new) that contains a commodity or product that falls under Annex I of the EUDR document.

An example is the procurement of wooden office furniture, wooden pallets and chairs with SAP Ariba Sourcing and Buying, or using SAP S/4HANA to source soybeans and timber.

SAP’s solutions to complying with EUDR

SAP offers a range of sourcing, reporting and due diligence solutions to help customers comply to EUDR. SAP Green Token is designed to address Article 9 of EUDR, while SAP Ariba Supplier Risk provides assessment, risk mitigation and monitoring solutions – and aims to help customers comply to Articles 10 and 11. Fig. 1 provides an overview of the solutions SAP and partners offer to help customers comply to EUDR.

Figure 1: How SAP Ariba works with SAP’s solutions to manage and mitigate deforestation risks for sourced commodities and other products containing declared items. *Suitable partners can be found on store.sap.com by searching for “EUDR”. ** Custom Fields or Custom Risk Categories (GA 2408, see SAP Help - Custom Risk Categories / SAP Help - Enable Custom Risk Categories).

SAP Green Token and Article 9

SAP Green Token is a supply chain compliance cloud solution that enables accounting of sustainable material movements with segregation or mass balancing and tokenization for trust and compliance with recognized standards and regulations. The primary EUDR compliance capability is the use of segregated material accounting (Fig. 2) and management of the due diligence statements for EUDR.

Figure 2: Input-output track and traceability of raw commodity materials across the value chain with tokenised sustainability information to the final product.

SAP S/4HANA sourcing and PO order line-item creation leads to SAP Green Token journey for a commodity that is impacted by EUDR. The inbound claims (Fig. 3) and orders for the commodities should be supplemented with the sustainability information from the supplier proving that the commodity has not been sourced from deforested land.

Figure 3: Inbound records of the raw commodities that are being sourced into the EU within SAP Green Token solution.

Depending on whether the organization is the primary importer into the EU or an organization further downstream within the EU, the due diligence statement information is automatically uploaded into the EUDR Traces Database. Subsequently, the generated reference number is captured back into SAP Green Token.

The next step requires the exporter or processor to undergo the “conversion” of the material into a percentage of waste (Fig. 4), recycled content, EUDR commodity content, etc. that perseveres the tokenisation process. The tokenisation process converts the raw material into a digital asset containing sustainability information which can then be passed down the supply chain via segregated-balanced accounting. In this use case, you may purchase a piece of furniture that is 40% timber coming from a non-EU country. For outgoing deliveries, SAP Green Token can automatically share this information with customers via email.

Figure 4: Conversion process where the percentage of materials are classified based on certain sustainability attributes so that they are fully accounted for.

The compliance process is not complete, as Articles 10 and 11 of the regulation need to be complied to. For this, a risk mitigation, assessment and management solution are needed. This is where SAP Ariba Supplier Management comes in.

During the outbounds process in SAP Green Token, due diligence statements can be tracked with the dashboard which can be seen below in Fig. 5.

Figure 5: Outbounds process where the POs for materials, satellite JSON files and their attached statements can be monitored towards compliance.

Using SAP Ariba Supplier Risk to conduct deforestation due diligence

SAP Ariba Supplier Risk supports the buyer with risk assessment, risk mitigation and risk monitoring activities for EUDR (Article 10 and 11). The supplier’s data can be added to SAP Ariba Supplier Risk through synchronization with an integrated ERP system, supplier data file import or by supplier requests as part of the onboarding process in SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance. To assess the EUDR risk exposure, suppliers can be segmented based on the commodity to identify suppliers in scope of EUDR. The supplier’s data can then be screened with intelligent web crawling solutions and 3rd party data providers that can be integrated via custom fields using the risk category information risk category can be developed within SAP Ariba Supplier Risk as a part of a maximum of 25 custom risk categories to feed in this data. Fig. 6: shows the screening and risk categorisation of the suppliers in the solution.

Figure 6: Showing the number of suppliers that are low, medium and high risk at deforestation practices. Buyer then conducts due diligence on these suppliers through further questionnaire assessments.

To achieve a holistic EUDR risk view of the supplier, the engagement request feature can be leveraged to obtain additional EUDR specific information directly from Tier-1 suppliers. In this context, customers have the flexibility to create their own questionnaires with EUDR related questions. When selecting suppliers for assessments, factors such as the country’s: incidence for deforestation, rate of deforestation, reports and claims of deforestation, impacted indigenous peoples from land clearing, corruption/political indexes, supply chain and plots of land cleared, risk of the product being mixed with other commodities at risk of deforestation, and any FLEGT timber licenses (if applicable). This forms a part of the risk assessment process.

In case a concrete risk has been identified (e.g. a high deforestation risk), it can be addressed by creating a finding that is associated with the engagement request. The risk mitigation is supported by the application Findings and Event Collaboration in SAP Ariba Supplier Risk. A finding is raised, reviewed and assigned to internal and external stakeholders such as the supplier risk manager, external stakeholders like the individuals from the supplier’s organization or other external parties for example, NGOs or auditors. They can collaborate on analyzing the finding and execute an action plan, like requesting further information from the supplier and planning audits on sites to proactively mitigate the risk.

Embedding EUDR due diligence into purchasing processes

 Buyers must be aware of the deforestation risk of suppliers whenever they need to take a decision. By embedding the deforestation risk category in SAP Ariba Sourcing and SAP Ariba Contracts buyers can determine suppliers with a low deforestation risk in a sourcing event or award contracts to suppliers that are best aligned with their deforestation risk appetites.

Figure 7: Buyers can extract up to 25 custom risk scores (and create a deforestation risk score) from SAP Ariba Supplier Risk into the SAP Ariba Contracts workspace and SAP Ariba Sourcing, with guided sourcing capabilities (from left to right).

EU Observatory and other resources

The EU provides resources (such as the EU observatory on deforestation in Fig. 8 and 9) to help buyers and importers of EUDR commodities to track and identify high risk countries of concern based on satellite, forest imagery that is continuously updated.  Importers of the high risk EUDR commodity can determine deforestation rates via this portal. You may be wondering; how does an importer legally import any form of wood into the EU? Wood that is harvested after the 29th of June 2023 will need to adhere to the EU Timber Regulation (EUTR), which is a regulation that aims to prevent imports of illegally logged timber products.

The EU will have a benchmarking system designating countries that are standard, low or high risk which will guide buyers in conducting further EUDR due diligence activities. The publication of the risk country profiles is planned to be made available from 30th of June 2025 and an existing country classification list can be accessed here.

Figure 8: The EU’s deforestation observatory showing commercial land clearing deforestation coverage and trends.

Figure 9: EU’s Observatory mask showing deforestation metrics such as coverage and incidence rates.

These are some of the publicly available tools provided by the EU that helps buyers monitor activities for deforestation. The buyer can take these metrics and conduct further due diligence on new imports for EUDR commodities and products through SAP Ariba Supplier Risk, SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance Management, and SAP Green Token on a yearly basis.

Conclusion

Chief Sustainability, Compliance and Procurement Officers face new regulatory, cross-border challenges they need to address in a time where environmental regulations are altering their sourcing behaviours to protect ecosystems. To facilitate this level of compliance, automated reporting, supplier assessments, and material track and trace solutions are needed to mitigate both supplier and deforestation risks. Solutions and new processes need to be established to ensure prevent confiscation of goods and avert fines. SAP Ariba and partners can help you comply by offering solutions to conduct due diligence, track and trace, due diligence statement reporting and risk management.

entry.title: Re: Problem viewing the entire structure of technical obj...
entry.content:
Hi Esnowy,

The structure in app Asset Viewer will only show all sub-nodes of the technical object. If you want to see the superior node, you may right click on the technical object -> Additional Functions -> Go to Superior Object.

Thanks,

Nick

entry.title: How to connect to SAP for Me with Single Sign On (SSO)
entry.content:
Hello,

 

I hope you are well,

 

I would like to know How to use single sign-on for S-user logins

Is there a tutorial available?

 

Thanks in advance for your response,

 

Have a nice day!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to connect to SAP for Me with Single Sign On (SSO)
entry.content: Please refer to KBA 1296615 - How to apply for and install the SAP Passport browser certificate - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: How to Restrict Access in SAP Fiori Screen Maintenanc...
entry.content: Hi Bethany, You can use Adapt UI for this. We have a new learning video on this technique - you can access it here https://learning.sap.com/videos/adapting-the-ui-of-list-report-apps-with-sap-fiori-for-sap-s-4hana

entry.title: copy co pa actual to plan version in sap s/4 hana private cloud
entry.content:
hi

I have a question about SAP CO PA(Costing based). I want to copy co pa actual to plan version. 

But I can not find T-code. Do I need to develop a CBO (Custom Business Object) program?

If not please let me know the standard T-code.

thanks Regards

entry.title: Re: Create custom tab and add fields in /SCWM/PACK transa...
entry.content:
Hi @Veera111 ,

You will get step by step info with picture and code in below press book...Sorry can not share due to copy right. just take screenshot for your reference 

https://www.sap-press.com/ewm-with-sap-s4hana-architecture-and-programming_5670/?srsltid=AfmBOorJ27oUwswWZid9jdgdhivN-8_bN1pqB537sChJx_dMsjbsaWb2

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Why a different landscape attribute is used to define the service.instance.id for BTP IS?
entry.content:
Why a different landscape attribute is used to define the service.instance.id for BTP IS? For RAW Outbound APIs, 
We see BTP's service.instance.id attribute as the externalId and not systemNumber. For the other S/4 HANA instances we have seen the service.instance.id being as systemNumber.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Why a different landscape attribute is used to define...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

For Cloud Service, the "externalId" is mapping to the attribute "service.instance.id".
For technical systems, the "systemNumber" is used.

Please refer to the SAP Cloud ALM API guide for details of the models mapping for Raw data outbound APIs.
https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/apis/raw-data-outbound-traces-api?locale=en-US, 
https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/apis/raw-data-outbound-logs?locale=en-US,

=> Models mapping

Best regards,
Peng

entry.title: Re: Returnable packaging owned by us to be used for vendo...
entry.content:
Hi @mfrankel,

Send Pallet  to Supplier -movement type: 502 (Custom Movement possible as per business need )- 

Receive pallet from Supplier- movement type: 501 (Custom Movement possible as per business need )

Send to Customer: movement type: 601 ( Not 601 similar to Custom Movement possible as per business need)

Receive from Customer: movement type: 602 ( Not 602 similar to Custom Movement possible as per business need)

Logistics is possible with above movement type however there are also finance consequence. Please take a loop to finance team also when you finalise 🙂

Please let us know if further questions.

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: Triggering workflow for Import supplier invoice from...
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a similar requirement to park the document once the supplier invoice is upload via app f3041.  How you solved the requirement.  Kindly help us know.

 

Regards

Anil

entry.title: Re: 供應商發票可否跨公司不同公司幣建立
entry.content:
Hello @jakeyin1 ,

In the same journal entry the transaction currency should be the same, it is not allow to use different transaction currencies in different line items. Based on the business requirement described, it is suggest to create separate journal entry for headquarter and subsidiary with different currencies.

Kind Regards,

entry.title: Infoporter Job Info - Hire date = FTSD
entry.content:
Hi,

Has anyone used Infoporter to migrate Job info into EC before?

I'm using it for the first time and it works except for the scenario when FTSD is the same as hire date.

In that scenario it gives me this error

You cannot create a job information record without the recruit record
for user xxxx. The first job information record must be the recruit
record.

I have configured the additional events as follows for the Hire Event

01 = event reason HIRNEW

Based on the SAP help document, it says here that it should follow the action type configured in the table.  Which if so it should be sending a HIRNEW event.  But based on the log its sending an evventReason DATACONV eventhough hire date is the same as FTSD date

entry.title: Re: How can I import the GL accounts for each Company code?
entry.content:
Hi @Jeremy_Deo,

Could you be a bit more specific about your customers's need ?

The client request uses a different GL code as follows:

Company A   GL Account 1001110001 Petty Cash  

Company B   GL Account 1002110001 Petty Cash

You want to know if it's possible to mass load G/L account through Managa Operating Chart of Accounts app, isn't it ?

I know the manage Operating Chart of Accounts app can mass upload GL by excel file.

But I want to know the template can input the company code for separate GL account by company code.

Sample

:Prepare the template for upload.

When I upload the template, the G/L account is assigned to two company codes.

But I want the G/L account to be assigned to only one company code.

Thanks,

Chanaporn S.

entry.title: Re: Item was saved inconsistently in PMR
entry.content:
Dear Ajit,

Thanks for your response!

Yes, I did it and found the message Log.

Regards

Vincent

entry.title: Re: 未本地化國家的公司幣別如何設定？
entry.content:
Hi @jakeyin1 ,

针对问题回复如下：

1.要如何將公司改為開曼?

针对已经创建的公司无法对国家和公司代码货币进行修改。如该公司未创建，可考虑使用本地化实施工具Configuration Localization Tool (CLT) 进行设置，具体的restriction请参考note: 3107866 - SAP Central Business Configuration : Configuration Localization Tool restrictions and limitations - SAP for Me

2.設定公司後要如何將公司改為USD?

如上所述，已经设定公司后无法修改公司代码货币。如有出具报表等需求，可考虑使用其他的货币类型实现。请参考：Create Additional Currency Types | SAP Help Portal

Kind Regards,

entry.title: Re: How to capture a Report for all the changes made to W...
entry.content: Hi @karthik1gobburu , could you please check in Configure object definition - Legislatively Sensitive Data Configuration - Work Order, to see what is configured in Data Protection and Privacy Configuration. You may need to create a change audit report - person data report to see the audit data.

entry.title: Configuring Only Necessary Permissions with Developer License in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi,
I'd like to know if, with the 'Sap Build Developer license', it's possible to configure only the necessary permissions for my app in SAP Build Apps — so that the generated .aab file includes only the permissions actually required by the app. My goal is to ensure compliance with Google Play’s guidelines, which emphasize requesting only permissions that are essential for app functionality.

Is this level of permission configuration supported with the Developer license?
Thanks in advance!

https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/build/pricing.html

entry.title: Re: Item was saved inconsistently in PMR
entry.content:
Dear Ajit,

The system also has an error prompt.

I am trying to find the storage type configuration.

Vincent.

entry.title: Re: Why AW01N show negative Net Book Value
entry.content:
Hi,

Thanks for your respond. What is your suggestion to fix this negative net book value?

 

thanks,

RTN

entry.title: Re: Item was saved inconsistently in PMR
entry.content:
Hi @freebing1973- Seems Master data is not correct example PSA Master data, use proper Goods Movement Bin and Warehouse Process Type... PSA Master Data, Storage types Tcodes: /SCWM/PSASTAGE

Guess: GoodsMvt Bin 0040-.... in storage type settings is "no good issue" checked so use proper data and settings as per below example

Examples

Please let us know if issues exist.

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: Enterprise Architecture as a Leadership Factor
entry.content:
Excellent blog and great content @Michael_Blaschke !
I am sure that the session was great!

entry.title: CAP application with multiple databases (HANA + PostgreSQL)?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Is it possible to configure a SAP CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) application to use more than one database?

For example:

  • For production, I want to have a primary database such as SAP HANA.

  • But I also want to be able to connect to a secondary database, like PostgreSQL, from within the same CAP application.

If this is possible, could you please provide an example of how the package.json configuration would look?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Item was saved inconsistently in PMR
entry.content:
Dear Ajit,

Yes, you are right!

I did it following your instruction. The problem was solved.

Thanks

Vincent

entry.title: Re: Advanced planning status revert to not relevant for planning
entry.content: Hi, If your Issue was resolved, Can you please comment your Solution here

entry.title: Re: How to cancel material document created by batch program
entry.content: If there is an authorization issue, you need to get the access with the help of Security team.  Share su53 screen shot to your security team who can provide the required access

entry.title: Re: Email Notifications: Token for Employee Title
entry.content:
@MariaCarlotaAbreu Here are a few list that provide lists of tokens (I doubt there is anything that would be available specifically for compensation that is not available for Performance, I believe the comp product team simply used the same exact framework than Performance for their notifications) :

https://me.sap.com/notes/2323087

 

 

Try EMP_JOBTITLE or EMP_TITLE maybe, who knows !

All the best, 
Xavier

entry.title: Re: CIAP@TDF: Baixa de fichas
entry.content:
Prezados,

Considerando a hipótese de baixa manual de fichas (Retirement Type = 04), não sendo portanto, vinculada à Nota Fiscal, caso ocorra uma falha operacional em que a baixa foi incorreta ou com quantidade parcial inconsistente, COMO desfazer a baixa. Existe BAPI para essa finalidade?

entry.title: SAP Open Source Webinar – Start your Architecture Journey with Open Sourced SAP Architecture Center
entry.content:
The recent launch of the SAP Architecture Center on GitHub helps you to build an efficient, reliable enterprise landscape and govern Reference Solutions Architectures.

The SAP Architecture Center offers a place that provides solution reference architectures to help drive the adoption of SAP Solutions. It contains solution diagrams, scenarios, service and component lists, examples and additional resources for a variety of different use case architectures. (read more). It is the natural move after openly providing the BTP Solution Diagrams via GitHub: Fabian Lehmann, BTP Product Manager SAP, will share with you the journey and milestones of these assets, which were presented last year in a similar webinar already and the collaboration as a crucial part for the darw.io integration as part of the SAP Architecture Center.

Become part of the architecture experts, who collaboratively improve the architecture journey of their company, customer, or within SAP.

Speakers: Pierre-Olivier Basseville, Product Manager SAP and Fabian Lehmann, BTP Product Manager SAP

Date and Time: July 9th, 2025, at 3:00 pm CEST/ 1:00 pm UTC/ 9:00 am EST

Host: SAP Open-Source Program Office

Register here.

entry.title: Re: Getting Started with SAP HANA Cloud | Video Tutorials
entry.content: Youtube links present in this page no longer available. Can it be fixed?

entry.title: Re: SAC - group accounts in AccountLookup ( parent - child )
entry.content: Is it possible to first use account formula to sum multiple accounts( including parents and leafs) , and then is ACCOUNTLOOKUP on measure?

entry.title: Re: CIAP@TDF: Baixa de fichas
entry.content:
Considerando legislação estadual fluminense que considera que fichas suspensas ao serem movimentados os respectivos bens a qualquer tempo até o prazo decadencial (60 meses da data de emissão da NFe) cumpra o requisito de início da apropriação (1/48), como configurar o SAP de modo que:
1. a ficha não seja cancelada automaticamente por decurso do quadriênio (48 meses), e ainda,
2. mantenha a apropriação da ficha até que se complete o quadriênio.
Ex.: Bem adquirido em 01.2020 é mantido em armazém até 12.2024, quando efetivamente é transferido (TRANSFER/862) para filial considerada produtiva (861) e então iniciada a apropriação com final do quadriênio em 11.2028.

entry.title: Re: Why AW01N show negative Net Book Value
entry.content:
Hi,

You can fix negative values by posting acquisition value in the assets with transaction code ABZON but before that check why its negative and what is the reason. As decision can be taken only if you know why its negative.

As i mentioned you can use ABZON to post the values in the asset but again you would need justification for the same. If its a development system, it fine but for production system, please take care and check the reasons.

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Re: Get filter values when right clicking on table
entry.content:
If user filter a hierarchy member by right click, you should get filtered member( parent or leaf) with this statement:

Table_1.getDataSource().getDimensionFilters("Dimension");

entry.title: Re: Profit Center Determination Error on FOC Sale Invoice...
entry.content: If we untick the field which defined as mandatory, then system will pass entry in blank profit center. Which will not correct.

entry.title: Re: Creating a new EHR for improving medical therapies!
entry.content: Discussing the creation of a new EHR, this blog offers insights into the innovation driving EHR Implementation. This perspective is valuable for understanding the evolving EHR System Implementation landscape. For a deeper dive into challenges associated with new EHR development, this might be insightful. As an expert in EHR Implementation, what emerging technologies do you believe will most significantly impact future EHR designs?

entry.title: MultiInput in a table with item binding doesn't add tokens on Initial copy paste from excel
entry.content:
Our Fiori application's Object Page has a custom Facet, which has a table with item binding. The table cells have a multiInput field and a ValueHelp button. Please find the image attached below


We have a customer requirement wherein customers wish to paste multiple values from an excel file directly into a multi Input field in a single paste, which gets pasted as Tab Separated values.

When tab separated values are pasted into any of the above Multi Input fields for the first time, the initial paste gets omitted and only the last value from the tab separated values appear in the field.

Additional Information:
1) Even with addValidator added to the MultiInput field, the Tokens only get added when item binding is removed from the table. (Note: This issue only occurs for the first paste of the input, before any tokens have been added).
This is the addValidator function

oMultiInput.addValidator(function(args){
					var text = args.text;
					return new sap.m.Token({key: text, text: text});
			});


2) Furthermore, the change and tokenUpdate events don't seem to get triggered for this paste. However, the liveChange event does get triggered, which could potentially be used for a workaround (Not preferred).

3) The tokenUpdate event : 

onTokenUpdate: function (oControlEvent) {
    if (oControlEvent.getParameters().type == "removed") {
        var oRemovedTokens = oControlEvent.getParameters().removedTokens[0];
        var oObjectPageView = this.getObjectPageView();
        var oModel = oObjectPageView.getModel();
        var oAttribModel = oObjectPageView.getModel("AttributeLocal");
        if (oRemovedTokens) {
            var tokenKey = oRemovedTokens.data("Key0");
            var attributeKey = oRemovedTokens.data("Key1");
            var dTeamID = oRemovedTokens.data("Key2");

            var sPath = "/C_RespyMgmtTeamAttributeTP(RespyMgmtTeamAttributeValueID=\'" + attributeKey + "\'" +
                ",RespyMgmtTeamID=\'" + dTeamID + "\'" + ",DraftUUID=guid\'" + tokenKey + "\'" + ",IsActiveEntity=false)";

            oModel.remove(sPath, {
                success: function (oData) {
                    var resArray = oAttribModel.getProperty("/RespyMgmtAttributeData");
                    var delFlag = false;
                    for (var k = 0; k < resArray.length; k++) {
                        if (resArray[k].DraftUUID === tokenKey && !delFlag) {
                            resArray.splice(k, 1);
                            oAttribModel.setProperty("/RespyMgmtAttributeData", resArray);
                            delFlag = true;
                        }
                    }
                    oModel.refresh(true);
                },
                error: function (oError) {
                }
            });
        }
    }
    if (oControlEvent.getParameters().type == "added") {
        for (var i = 0; i < oControlEvent.getParameters().addedTokens.length; i++) {
            var oMultiInput = oControlEvent.getSource();
            var oAttributeValue = oControlEvent.getParameters().addedTokens[i].getAggregation("customData")[0].getValue().value1;
            oMultiInput.setValue("");
            if (oAttributeValue) {
                var oAttributeName = oMultiInput.data("coords0");
                var attributeUnit = oMultiInput.data("Key5");
                var attributeCurrency = oMultiInput.data("Key6");
                // code for supporting manual input of tokens for all operators
                var oTokenParser = new TokenParser();
                oTokenParser.addKeyField({
                    key: oAttributeName,
                    label: oAttributeName,
                    type: "string"
                });
                oTokenParser.setDefaultOperation("EQ");
                oTokenParser.associateInput(oMultiInput);
                var oNewToken = oMultiInput._validateToken({
                    text: oAttributeValue
                });

                var temp = 0;
                var oRange = oNewToken.data().range;
                var option = oRange.operation;
                var Low = oRange.value1;
                Low = Low.toUpperCase(Low);
                var High = oRange.value2;
                if (High) {
                    High = High.toUpperCase(High);
                }
                var Exclude = oRange.exclude;
                if (option === "Contains" || option === "StartsWith" || option === "EndsWith") {
                    temp = this.checkEscapeCharacter(Low);
                    Low = this.operatorChangeForRanges(Low, option, temp);
                    option = "CP";
                }
                // end of code for supporting manual input of tokens for all operators
                var oObjectPageView = this.getObjectPageView();
                var oModel = oObjectPageView.getModel();
                var oAttribModel = oObjectPageView.getModel("AttributeLocal");
                var oBindingContext = oObjectPageView.getBindingContext();
                var sPath = oBindingContext.getPath();
                sPath = sPath + "/to_Attribute";
                var DraftUUID = oBindingContext.getObject().DraftUUID;
                var data = {
                    ParentDraftUUID: DraftUUID,
                    RespyMgmtReferenceAttribName: oAttributeName,
                    RespyMgmtAttributeOption: option,
                    RespyMgmtAttributeSign: Exclude ? "E" : "I",
                    RespyMgmtAttributeLowValue: Low,
                    RespyMgmtAttributeHighValue: High,
                    RespyMgmtAttributeUnit: attributeUnit,
                    RespyMgmtAttributeCurrencyKey: attributeCurrency
                };
                oModel.create(sPath, data, {
                    success: function (oData, oResponse) {
                        oData.RespyMTeamAttributeIsEditable = true;
                        var resArray = oAttribModel.getProperty("/RespyMgmtAttributeData");
                        resArray.push(oData);
                        oAttribModel.setProperty("/RespyMgmtAttributeData", resArray);
                        oModel.refresh(true);
                    },
                    error: function (oError) { }
                });

            }
        }
    }
},

4) The change event : 

onInputAttribute: function (oControlEvent) {
			var oMultiInput = oControlEvent.getSource();
			var oAttributeValue = oControlEvent.getParameters().value;
			oMultiInput.setValue("");
			if (oAttributeValue) {
				var oAttributeName = oMultiInput.data("coords0");
				var attributeUnit = oMultiInput.data("Key5");
				var attributeCurrency = oMultiInput.data("Key6");
				// code for supporting manual input of tokens for all operators
				var oTokenParser = new TokenParser();
				oTokenParser.addKeyField({
					key: oAttributeName,
					label: oAttributeName,
					type: "string"
				});
				oTokenParser.setDefaultOperation("EQ");
				oTokenParser.associateInput(oMultiInput);
				var oNewToken = oMultiInput._validateToken({
					text: oAttributeValue
				});

				var temp = 0;
				var oRange = oNewToken.data().range;
				var option = oRange.operation;
				var Low = oRange.value1;
				Low = Low.toUpperCase(Low);
				var High = oRange.value2;
				if (High) {
					High = High.toUpperCase(High);
				}
				var Exclude = oRange.exclude;
				if (option === "Contains" || option === "StartsWith" || option === "EndsWith") {
					temp = this.checkEscapeCharacter(Low);
					Low = this.operatorChangeForRanges(Low, option, temp);
					option = "CP";
				}
				// end of code for supporting manual input of tokens for all operators
				var oObjectPageView = this.getObjectPageView();
				var oModel = oObjectPageView.getModel();
				var oAttribModel = oObjectPageView.getModel("AttributeLocal");
				var oBindingContext = oObjectPageView.getBindingContext();
				var sPath = oBindingContext.getPath();
				sPath = sPath + "/to_Attribute";
				var DraftUUID = oBindingContext.getObject().DraftUUID;
				var data = {
					ParentDraftUUID: DraftUUID,
					RespyMgmtReferenceAttribName: oAttributeName,
					RespyMgmtAttributeOption: option,
					RespyMgmtAttributeSign: Exclude ? "E" : "I",
					RespyMgmtAttributeLowValue: Low,
					RespyMgmtAttributeHighValue: High,
					RespyMgmtAttributeUnit: attributeUnit,
					RespyMgmtAttributeCurrencyKey: attributeCurrency
				};

				oModel.create(sPath, data, {
					success: function (oData, oResponse) {
						oData.RespyMTeamAttributeIsEditable = true;
						var resArray = oAttribModel.getProperty("/RespyMgmtAttributeData");
						resArray.push(oData);
						oAttribModel.setProperty("/RespyMgmtAttributeData", resArray);
						oModel.refresh(true);
					},
					error: function (oError) {}
				});

			}
		}

entry.title: Re: Using standard plans and "Generative AI hub" menu is still missing.
entry.content: Hi, it appears that you are using Standard service Plan of AI Core. You need to use Extended plan of AI Core to use GenAI hub

entry.title: Job title not appearing on external job posting on careers site
entry.content:
Hi

On posting jobs to external careers site, the job title is not appearing on the external careers site.

Everything else is but not the title.

On exporting the jobs in csv from command center , we noticed that all job titles are present.

Also in the external posting preview we can see the title.

Why is it then the job title is missing only on the external careers site posting?

Pls help.

Thanks,

Nimesha

entry.title: Practices of customizing the events on Employee Central
entry.content:
Hi, experts,

Our customers need to add some events to meet the HR actions in Japan.

But refer to this document, it seems like the events cannot be customized.

Events in Employee Central | SAP Help Portal

Does anyone have some practices for this?

Flora.W

entry.title: Re: What is the importance of Releasing the maintenance o...
entry.content:
Hello  

After creation of maintenace order there is an background configuration which can be done to release the Purchase requestion (PR) whether to release PR immediately or after the order got released .

Plant maintenance and customer service -> maintenance and service processing -> maintenance and service order -> functions and settings for order types -> define change documents , collective purchase requestions,MRP relevance 

if you set Req/PurRq 

1. Immediately : The purchase requestion will be released immediately and the purchase team can make the PR to PO but they cannot do or make goods reciept for the material document number the system will throw an error like the order is still in create status .

2. Release : The purchase requestions set to release then unless the order needs to released or else the purchase requestion will not be released and the purchase team cannot do PO as well as Goods Reciept (MIGO) .

And to create PO the order can be in CRTD -Created status but for doing goods reciept in MIGO then the Order needs to be released .

Regards,

Neeraj

entry.title: Re: Support for SAP HANA DB on Linux Suse SLES 15 SP4
entry.content:
Hi Eason,

According to the Note 2235581, SLES 15 SP4 also support HANA 2.0 SPS05. However, on the SLES 15 SP5, it says that SLES 15 SP5 support HANA 2.0 SPS5 starting with revision 59.08. Does this mean I cannot update HANA 2.0 SPS05 revision 59.8 or 59.12 when the OS version is SLES 15 SP4?

 We are running HANA 2.0 SPS05 revision 59 on SLES 15 SP4. We plan update to HANA 2.0 SPS08 and SLES 15 SP6. Do we need update to HANA 2.0 SPS5 revision 59.8 and SLES 15 SP5 as an intermediate step?

Thank you for your help.

entry.title: Re: Discrepancy in planned effort in "Plan Customer Proje...
entry.content:
Hi,

if planned effort is consider as staffing effort value.

with which value, ETC is calculated? as per this query explain in help portal i see as below, 
EstimateToCompleteEffort =Indicates the number of hours needed to complete the work planned in a work package or project. (ie, planned hours).

so for ETC calculation, which value this query picks up.

Based on ETC, now we need to calculate EAC as well. so all the calculation in the report depends on planned effort.

hope for your reply

Thanks,

entry.title: Accessing SAP Datasphere from ABAP System (S/4HANA OP)
entry.content:
Hi,

Our requirement is to access data exposed in SAP Datasphere from an ABAP system (S/4HANA OP) via ODBC. Going through the help Consume Data in Power BI and Other Clients, Tools, and Apps via ODBC/JDBC | SAP Help Portal it is not clear whether the following step is from ABAP system perspective or not. 

  • Install the SAP HANA CLIENT 2.0 (see Download and Install the SAP HANA Client in the SAP HANA Cloud documentation).

Our use case is to read certain data in SAP Datasphere from a Fiori app hosted in the ABAP system. This Fiori app is used by developers internally in SAP. Hence there cannot be a prerequisite that something is installed in every developer's laptop. I might be wrong in my understanding. Hence requesting clarity and guidance here. 

Regards,

Madhura Lobo

entry.title: Location Entry Date
entry.content:
🚨 The Case of the Missing Location Entry Dates! 🚨

We recently migrated data for around 5,000 employees from another HR system into SAP SuccessFactors. Everything seemed smooth at first…
Until we discovered something strange:
💡 The "Location Entry Date" field was missing from their Job Info records!

We thought it would be a quick fix — just go in, add the missing date, and save.
But nope! ❌ The system either rejected the change or didn’t reflect it at all.

So we reached out to our SAP consultant, and this was the response:

"You can’t add a Location Entry Date for a future date. It only updates when an employee is hired or when they actually move to a new location."

Fair enough, but we’re now stuck with thousands of employees without this key field populated.

🧠 Has anyone faced this before? How did you fix it?

entry.title: Re: CAP application with multiple databases (HANA + PostgreSQL)?
entry.content: Yes, you can absolutely configure a SAP CAP application to use more than one database. While your primary data models will typically reside in the default database (like SAP HANA for production), you can define additional data sources in your package.json to connect to other databases such as PostgreSQL. You'd specify separate kind and credentials for each database connection within the cds.requires section. Then, in your CAP services, you can use cds.connect.to('yourSecondaryDbName') to interact with the secondary database, often via raw SQL or by modeling its entities if you need more structured interaction. This allows for flexibility in integrating with diverse data sources.

entry.title: Re: IP30 doesn't reschedule maintenance plan as there is...
entry.content:
Hello ,

Generally we use IP30 to mass schedule the maintenance plans and to set it for backgorund job and the last TECO date will not appear or the system will not ask for the last TECO date but when it comes to IP10 you can able see there is an option in Start in cycle that means the system will pop up a screen in which the last completion or else the last TECO completion date on which the maintenance order is completed or else we can also use the function offset too.

 

Regards.

entry.title: Why Is My SAP AI Core Training Job Taking Much Longer Than Reported Duration?
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I'm currently working on a machine learning pipeline using SAP AI Core, where I trigger a training job via a workflow (training.yml) that pulls a training script and executes it.

However, I've noticed that there is significant configuration time before and after the actual training begins. The model training itself takes the same amount of time as before, but there are additional waiting periods both before the training starts and after it completes. For example, in the image below, although the duration is shown as 1 minute 22 seconds, the entire execution took around 10 minutes to complete.


Is there a recommended way to optimize or reduce this setup/configuration time?

here is my training.yml code:

 

apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: WorkflowTemplate
metadata:
  name: aicore-anomaly-training
  annotations:
    scenarios.ai.sap.com/name: "Aicore Anomaly Detection Scenario"
    scenarios.ai.sap.com/description: "Pipeline for anomaly detection"
    executables.ai.sap.com/name: "Anomaly AI Core Model"
    executables.ai.sap.com/description: "Trains anomaly detection models"
    artifacts.ai.sap.com/anomalymodel.kind: "model"
  labels:
    scenarios.ai.sap.com/id: "aicore-anomaly"
    ai.sap.com/version: "1.0"
spec:
  imagePullSecrets:
    - name: tanmay-credentials
  entrypoint: anomaly-pipeline

  arguments:
    parameters:
      - name: GUID
      - name: PLANT
      - name: NODE_PARAMETERS
      - name: NODE_ID
      - name: ALGORITHMS

  templates:
    - name: anomaly-pipeline
      outputs:
        artifacts:
          - name: anomaly-aicore
            globalName: anomalymodel
            path: /app/model
            archive:
              none: {}
      container:
        image: docker.io/tanmay471/anamoly_train_optimised:01
        command: ["/bin/sh", "-c"]
        env:
          - name: GUID
            value: "{{workflow.parameters.GUID}}"
          - name: PLANT
            value: "{{workflow.parameters.PLANT}}"
          - name: NODE_PARAMETERS
            value: "{{workflow.parameters.NODE_PARAMETERS}}"
          - name: NODE_ID
            value: "{{workflow.parameters.NODE_ID}}"
          - name: ALGORITHMS
            value: "{{workflow.parameters.ALGORITHMS}}"
        args:
          - "python /app/src/main.py"

entry.title: Fiori Reuse Library
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We have an interesting problem.

We developed a Fiori app using Business Application Studio (BAS) and integrated the Reuse Component for Change Documents following the provided instructions (How to Use the Fiori Reuse Library | SAP Help Portal)

The app runs perfectly in BAS. However, after deployed it to the Public Cloud. The apps failed to run, showing the error:""
My source code Reuse Library: Reuse_library 
Am i missing any permissions to use Reuse Library in Customizing.

Any insights or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition  

SAPUI5  

SAP Fiori

entry.title: Re: Eclipse(HANA Development): Error when searching for a...
entry.content: Hello, The fix of the issue should be included in the latest Eclipse 2025-03 (4.35), use https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/2025-03 or https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/latest to download it.

entry.title: Re: Universal Journal Import - Cannot Run UJ Task in Peri...
entry.content:
Hi Eyal, Trond here -- long time! Hope all is well 🙂
I've checked in my system, which has the same setup, and got the same result. I have not directly encountered this issue myself, but after some research, I found this (see below). Probably not the answer you were looking for, but it's a least an explanation.
Rgds Trond

The "Release Universal Journal" task is not available in periods 13-16 in SAP Group Reporting because these periods are considered special periods and are not directly integrated into the standard consolidation process. Data entered in these special periods in the S/4HANA system is automatically moved to Period 12 in Group Reporting. 
 
Here's a more detailed explanation:
  • Special Periods:
    Periods 13-16 are designated as special periods within SAP S/4HANA and are not directly linked to standard consolidation periods in Group Reporting. 
     
  • Data Handling:
    Data posted in these special periods is automatically mapped to Period 12 in Group Reporting. This ensures that all financial data is available for consolidation within the standard reporting framework. 
     
  • Release Universal Journal Functionality:
    The "Release Universal Journal" task in Group Reporting is primarily designed to transfer data from the Universal Journal to the consolidation environment. It's not intended to handle special periods, as their data is already integrated into Period 12. 
     
  • No Direct Integration:
    There's no direct integration of special periods with the "Release Universal Journal" task because the tool is designed to automatically move data to Period 12, according to SAP Community.
